frameworks.name: serial dependence (temporal-correlation prior)
frameworks.one_line: perceptual decisions biased toward previous stimuli given a prior of temporal continuity

frameworks.name: sensory adaptation
frameworks.one_line: repulsive biases away from recent stimulus history

frameworks.name: temporal context tuning
frameworks.one_line: broader temporal statistics shape both attraction and repulsion in perception

core_question: Does true temporal correlation in stimulus and decision history modulate serial dependence in orientation perception?
key_assumptions: serial dependence reflects a prior assuming temporal correlation in sensory input
stimulus history and decision history act on distinct timescales
observers' orientation reproductions can dissociate attractive from repulsive biases
manipulations: temporal correlation in stimulus history within a trial
temporal correlation in decision history across trials
key_findings: increasing temporal correlation in stimulus history produced adaptation-like repulsive biases
increasing temporal correlation in decision history reduced attractive biases
a clear dissociation emerged between stimulus-history and decision-history effects
interpretations: temporal correlation enhances the visual system's discriminative ability
broader temporal context plays a fundamental role in shaping perception and decisions
